concerns.text: Task T020 specifies using 'Clopper-Pearson 95% CI' for adaptive replication. This violates FR-004 and Constitution Principle VII, which explicitly mandate 'bootstrap resampling methods' for computing 95% confidence intervals. The plan's 'Complexity Tracking' section justifies this deviation, but the task list must not silently implement a different algorithm than the spec requires without an explicit amendment or staging note in the spec itself.

Task T027 describes fitting a GLM to predict '|p - 0.05|' but omits the requirement to model the 'log-transformed p-value distribution' as mandated by FR-006. This is a silent relaxation of the functional requirement, dropping half of the regression analysis scope defined in the spec.

Task T026 covers aggregation and CI computation but lacks a specific verification step to measure 'stability' of Type I error rates across sample sizes as required by Success Criterion SC-002. The spec requires measuring stability to confirm robustness, but the task only lists generating data and computing CIs, leaving the specific metric for 'stability' (e.g., variance analysis or trend test) undefined in the deliverable.

The spec (US-2) requires 'automatically extend[ing] the run' dynamically based on CI width. Tasks T020 and T022 describe the logic and batch saving but do not explicitly include a task for the 'adaptive control loop' that monitors CI width in real-time and triggers additional replicates. T022's mention of 'intermediate results' suggests a batch process, which may not satisfy the dynamic extension requirement of FR-002.
